title: Responsive Breakpoint Sweep Loop
summary: Systematically fixes layout, content, and interaction issues across breakpoints.
steps: Define representative breakpoints and states including empty, loading, error, and long-content cases.
Capture each page or component at each breakpoint using the same browser and data setup.
Rank visible issues by task impact, not by cosmetic annoyance alone.
Fix the highest-impact issue and recapture the affected matrix.
Repeat until all acceptance criteria pass or remaining issues are explicitly accepted.
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Responsive Breakpoint Sweep Loop for [pages_or_components, breakpoints, acceptance_criteria].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Define representative breakpoints and states including empty, loading, error, and long-content cases.
- Capture each page or component at each breakpoint using the same browser and data setup.
- Rank visible issues by task impact, not by cosmetic annoyance alone.
- Fix the highest-impact issue and recapture the affected matrix.
- Repeat until all acceptance criteria pass or remaining issues are explicitly accepted.

Quality gates before completion:
- breakpoint matrix complete
- task-blocking issues fixed first
- long content tested
- recaptures prove no regression

Stop when one of these is true:
- matrix passes
- accepted issues documented
- budget ends

Finish with:
- screenshot matrix
- issue ranking
- fix diff
- final capture

Escalate for human approval before: accepting task-blocking layout issue, changing content hierarchy.
